events.description: Chief of Branch of Realty H. M. Critchfield issued memorandum to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee, enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 office letter
Bureau memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy for issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to Superintendents
Billings Area Office transmitted subsequent fee patent policy statement to Superintendents
Transmittal of copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy to Superintendent, Northern Cheyenne
Office memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16 and subsequent policy statements
Billings Area Office memorandum to Superintendents regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands, quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955
Central Office memorandum regarding disposition of submarginal land grazing fees and amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
Billings Area Office memorandum transmitting committee reports from Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area at Sioux City, Iowa
Billings Area Office memorandum to all Superintendents regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area, June 13-15, 1955, with committee appointments and reports on land operations, conservation, and reporting systems
Committee appointed on June 14, 1955 at Land Operations Conference; made preliminary report to conference on June 14
Federal Register amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of submarginal land grazing fees in special deposits

events.location: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule Reservations
events.description: Range inventory survey made by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; now considered obsolete due to radical change in concept of scientific range inventory
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist, transferred from Standing Rock to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin
Central Office received numerous telegrams, letters, and postal cards objecting to or inquiring about fee patent policy stated in Bureau memorandum of May 16

financial_transactions.description: Grazing fees collected for submarginal land grazing privileges held in special deposit pending legislation to transfer lands and fees to respective tribes
After August 10, 1955 amendment, submarginal land grazing fees to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719, Rental of Land Not Otherwise Classified
Construction charges required under 25 CFR 151 when restricted Indian land sold to non-Indian; delinquent operation and maintenance charges deducted from land sale proceeds per Bid Form 5-114

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee as equitable investment for lessees of Indian lands

relationships.subject: K. S. Engel
relationships.context: Land Operations Officer, served as committee chairman at Land Operations Conference

relationships.subject: Rex Carey
relationships.context: Agency Soil Scientist, transferred from Standing Rock to Pine Ridge

relationships.subject: Bill Smith
relationships.context: Soil Scientist on educational leave, to return to Rosebud

relationships.subject: Bureau of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: BLM called attention to issues with patent requests; both agencies process patents in fee

relationships.subject: Billings Area Office
relationships.context: Area office issuing directives and memoranda to superintendents

relationships.subject: Commissioner of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: Commissioner issued fee patent policy memorandum to area directors

relationships.subject: Soil Conservation Service
relationships.context: SCS performed range inventory surveys under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; planned formal soil survey work plan for Shannon County

relationships.subject: Indian women married to white men
relationships.context: Committee recommended declaring Indian women married to white men ineligible for grazing allocations as non-Indian husbands effectively receive the subsidy

relationships.subject: Aberdeen Area Office
relationships.object: North Dakota, South Dakota, Nebraska reservations
relationships.context: Aberdeen Area comprises approximately 6.5 million acres of Indian lands on reservations in these states

relationships.subject: Act of March 7, 1928
relationships.context: Creates first lien against irrigable lands for unpaid construction, operation and maintenance costs; lien must be recited in patents

relationships.subject: 25 CFR 71.27
relationships.context: Regulation governing disposition of grazing fees on submarginal lands, amended August 10, 1955

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for easements/rights of way in all patent requests to allow expeditious handling
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum distributed; Billings Area Office forwarded to Superintendents on January 6, 1955

correspondence.subject: Rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Realty staff to obtain information on rights of way crossing restricted Indian lands and include in transmittals with patent applications or public sale advertisements
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued with copy of December 13, 1954 office letter enclosed

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by policy in May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement; exceptions to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Policy statement distributed; temporary manual release to be issued incorporating substance of memorandum and statement

correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy - copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55
correspondence.outcome: Transmittal sent with copy of memorandum

correspondence.recipient: Superintendent and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Any funds in special deposits under this category to be deposited to Treasury before end of calendar year
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Comments requested on conclusions reached by several committees
correspondence.outcome: Committee reports from Sioux City conference distributed with request for comment

correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Each Superintendent to establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment requirements before land sale transactions are finally completed
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued following Bureau auditors calling attention to failures to obtain payment for accrued charges

correspondence.sender_title: Bureau of Indian Affairs Central Office
correspondence.subject: Disposition of submarginal land grazing fees and amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
correspondence.action_requested: Funds held in special deposits to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719; future revenues to be deposited to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Quoted in Billings Area Office memorandum of November 29, 1955

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted, creating first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects for unpaid construction, operation and maintenance costs; directs lien to be recited in patents

Pending legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; special deposit of fees was in support of this legislation; August 1955 amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 noted legislation could provide for crediting deposited funds to tribe account if desired

Amendment published in Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777, deleting reference to deposit of submarginal land grazing fees in special deposits; fees to be deposited to Treasury general fund revenue receipt accounts
